content:
Since we already have our own BoxExt trait, I was thinking if we could drop the new_uninit feature and just implement it ourselves. Box implements it like this:

    pub unsafe fn assume_init(self) -> Box<T, A> {
        let (raw, alloc) = Box::into_raw_with_allocator(self);
        unsafe { Box::from_raw_in(raw as *mut T, alloc) }
    }

And we could just do that ourselves.

The other functions also look simple enough to do.

What's the likelihood of this feature being stabilised soon...ish?

If it's hopeless, then I'm onboard with dropping it.

My impression (though admittedly I haven't looked at its history/comments) is that it isn't very controversial so perhaps we could just push for stabilisation?
it might be blocked on keyword generics, but I dont know...

Last I heard the libs team was looking for a way to have something like a builder pattern rather than all the permutations of new, new_uninit, new_uninit_zeroed, try_new, etc. I proposed an incomplete API somewhere (libs team repo?) but it didn’t go anywhere

Wedson Almeida Filho said:

What's the likelihood of this feature being stabilised soon...ish?

Unfortunately I think the answer here is not to count on it. Though it doesn’t hurt to at least direct some focus there
Here is that by the way https://github.com/rust-lang/wg-allocators/issues/90. This isn't listed as a concern for new_uninit but it came up on Zulip iirc

I guess maybe nobody has proposed just moving forward with a minimal stabilization, I'll bring that up even minimal means 4 new functions per smart pointer, I guess I'd rather push for some motion on a builder

I think it is not really a feature that we need to rely upon, since its implementation is so simple. if the feature needs more dev time, then we can just not rely on it and the rust folks can develop at their own pace. we always have the option of later using the new way.
Yeah, agreed. Just something that has been an annoyance for a long long time, not only here
I added a couple notes and moved it down to the next tier in #2.

On the topic of features we need, we can probably do without feature(impl_trait_in_assoc_type) as well. I looked over the use-case in @Andreas Hindborg's code, but that should be solvable by moving the impl Trait to the return type of new_request_data instead of using an associated type.
Thanks Alice -- Andreas, if you confirm you don't need it, then I will move it down too. Thanks!
Using impl Trait in the return type of trait methods was stabilized together with async fn in traits in december 2023.
Down to "Low priority". I guess we could just remove it too.
